methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
FlyBy Robotics is a Seed-stage defense and security robotics company founded by Jason Lu with $5M+ in total funding. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI, spanning Seed through Series A stages in the same sector.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $5M raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $12M. Peer-median analysis references four same-sector comparables: Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed), Ares Industries at $150M (Series A), Vatn Systems at $300M (Series A), and Stark Defence at $500M (Series A). The median across this set is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $11M (low) to $259M (high), with a median implied value of $119M. The wide range reflects the stage gap between FlyBy's Seed position and the predominantly Series A comparable set. Key uncertainties include the company's current technical maturity, revenue or traction metrics, and capital efficiency relative to peers. The peer-median approach likely overstates valuation given FlyBy's earlier stage; stage-progression may understate given sector dynamics. A post-seed financing round with clearer milestones would narrow this range materially.
